title: Apple Crumble

overview.description: A classic British pudding of soft baked apples beneath a crisp buttery crumble topping.

ingredients: 1kg Bramley apples (or cooking apples), peeled, cored and sliced
method: Preheat the oven to 180°C fan (200°C conventional).
Place the sliced apples into a baking dish.
Sprinkle over the caster sugar and cinnamon, if using.
Mix gently to coat the apples evenly.
Place the flour and butter into a bowl.
Rub together with your fingertips until the mixture resembles coarse breadcrumbs.
Stir in the light brown sugar.
Scatter the crumble evenly over the apples without pressing it down.
Bake for 40–45 minutes until the topping is golden brown and the apples are bubbling around the edges.
Allow to stand for 5–10 minutes before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Apple crumble became popular during the Second World War when rationing made pastry too expensive to use regularly.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to make a traditional crumble topping and balance tart cooking apples with a sweet, crisp topping.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A timeless British pudding that's inexpensive, comforting and a brilliant way to use seasonal apples.
chapman-tips: Bramley apples give the best balance of sweetness and sharpness.
Keep the butter cold for the lightest crumble.
Don't compact the topping—keeping it loose makes it crisp.
A handful of porridge oats or chopped nuts can be added to the topping for variation.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze either unbaked or baked and cooled.
notes.leftovers: Reheat in the oven to restore the crisp topping.
